page: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-intel is a shared-core virtual private server belonging to the High Performance family. It features a single shared vCPU based on the x86_64 architecture, powered by an Intel Xeon Cascadelake processor running at 2.0 GHz. This single-core, single-thread configuration is paired with 1.0 GB of system memory and a local 25 GB NVMe SSD for storage. The CPU supports hardware-accelerated instructions including AVX, AVX-512 (specifically AVX-512 Foundation and AVX-512 VNNI), AES cryptography, and FMA.

Benchmark results indicate that the server delivers average single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1421.08. However, due to its single-core shared allocation, multi-core performance is limited, scoring identically at 1421.08 in stress-ng multi-core tests. Memory performance is also constrained, with a PassMark memory score of 784.86 and large-block read and write bandwidths of 9.16 GB/sec and 7.89 GB/sec respectively. Redis SET operations reach 1.58 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is recorded at 1.37 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, this instance represents a highly budget-friendly, entry-level tier. The shared CPU model and minimal memory footprint trade off raw computing power and memory bandwidth for cost efficiency. It is best suited for lightweight, non-resource-intensive workloads such as basic web serving, small development or staging environments, simple automation scripts, and low-traffic applications that do not require sustained high CPU or memory throughput.
description: Vultr vhp-1c-1gb-intel is an entry-level, shared-core virtual server in the High Performance family. It is configured with a single shared vCPU from an Intel Xeon Cascadelake processor running at 2.0 GHz, 1.0 GB of RAM, and a 25 GB NVMe SSD. The x86_64 CPU supports AVX-512 and AES instructions. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1421.08, though its memory bandwidth and multi-core capabilities remain in the lower tier due to the shared, single-thread resource allocation. Redis SET operations achieve 1.58 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 1.37 GB/sec. This server offers a highly cost-efficient profile for lightweight workloads, making it suitable for basic web serving, small-scale development environments, and simple background tasks.

tagline: An entry-level shared-core virtual server combining a single Intel Xeon vCPU with fast NVMe storage for lightweight, budget-friendly workloads.

1.0 GB of system memory and 25 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
